title: podman container prune not working as documentation claims
body:
### Issue Description

Man page of podman container prune  claims        
podman container prune removes all stopped containers from local storage.

I have a heap of dead processes, but podman container prune remove only two of them, most others remain untouched, e.g.

d2f1fbbd7d37  docker.io/library/couchdb:latest                /opt/couchdb/bin/...  6 months ago  Exited (130) 6 months ago  0.0.0.0:5984->5984/tcp, 4369/tcp, 9100/tcp


podman inspect d2f1fbbd7d37:

"State": {
               "OciVersion": "1.2.0",
               "Status": "exited",
               "Running": false,
               "Paused": false,
               "Restarting": false,
               "OOMKilled": false,
               "Dead": false,
               "Pid": 0,
               "ExitCode": 130,
               "Error": "",
               "StartedAt": "2026-01-03T02:26:26.54791713+01:00",
               "FinishedAt": "2026-01-03T02:59:45.962556718+01:00",
               "CheckpointedAt": "0001-01-01T00:00:00Z",
               "RestoredAt": "0001-01-01T00:00:00Z"
          },


Usually, a container not running (here: Running=false) is considered as stopped, and thus, it should be removed. But it doesn't. 


The containers can easily be removed with  podman rm

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. have a heap of stopped processes
2. podman container prune
3. podman ps -a


### Describe the results you received

containers still here

### Describe the results you expected

containers gone
